fund.name: Aditya Birla Sun Life Nifty Midcap 150 Index Fund-Direct Growth
fund.fund_house: Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC Limited

allocations.rationale: Core mid-cap index fund for growth with lowest fees (0.2%), diversified exposure to 150 quality mid-sized companies

fund.name: BANDHAN SMALL CAP FUND - DIRECT PLAN GROWTH

allocations.rationale: High-performing small cap fund with strong track record (31% 3-year returns), satellite allocation for aggressive growth

fund.name: WhiteOak Capital Flexi Cap Fund Direct Plan-Growth

allocations.rationale: Flexi cap fund for broad market exposure across large, mid, small caps with lower volatility and good risk-adjusted returns

fund.name: Motilal Oswal S&P 500 Index Fund - Direct Plan Growth
fund.fund_house: Motilal Oswal Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: US market diversification through S&P 500 index fund for global exposure and currency diversification

plan.setup_phase:
Step 1: Open direct mutual fund accounts on platforms like Groww, Kuvera or Zerodha
Step 2: Set up monthly SIPs totaling ₹60,000 across these 4 funds
Step 3: Invest your existing ₹20 lakh corpus as lumpsum across these funds according to the allocation
Step 4: Continue SIPs while at sea and make additional lump sum investments when ashore
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Check if SIPs are running smoothly and funds are performing as expected
After 1 year: Review performance against benchmarks and assess if allocations need adjustment
Every 2 years: Rebalance portfolio to maintain target allocations
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Rebalance annually or when allocations drift more than 5% from targets. Sell overweight funds and buy underweight funds to get back to 40% mid-cap, 30% small-cap, 20% flexi-cap, 10% US index
plan.rationale: This aggressive 90% equity plan suits Captain Haddock's risk appetite and 20-year horizon. The mid-cap index core provides stable growth, small-cap satellite boosts returns, flexi-cap adds diversification, and US exposure provides global diversification. With your merchant navy income pattern, the SIP+lumpsum approach matches your cash flow perfectly.
risks: Stock markets can drop 30-40% in bad years — your portfolio value will temporarily decline
Small caps are more volatile and can see bigger swings than larger companies
US market exposure adds currency risk — rupee-dollar fluctuations affect returns
All equity funds are subject to market risk — no guaranteed returns
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth and inflation

aps.reasoning: The investment plan includes two index funds (Aditya Birla Sun Life Nifty Midcap 150 Index and Motilal Oswal S&P 500 Index), which constitute 50% of the equity allocation (40% + 10%), resulting in a passive_instrument_fraction of 0.5. The turnover_score is 0.5 because rebalancing is recommended annually or when allocations drift more than 5%, and formal reviews occur every 2 years, indicating moderate turnover rather than strict buy-and-hold. The cost_emphasis_score is 0.4 as expense ratios are mentioned only for the mid-cap index fund (noted as 0.2%), but not emphasized as a primary selection criterion across other funds, which have higher expense ratios (1.0-1.2%) without critique. The research_vs_cost_score is 0.3 because fund selection appears driven more by performance (e.g., '31% 3-year returns') and risk-adjusted metrics than by broad market exposure without research; there's no indication of deep stock-level analysis, but active fund choices suggest some manager/fund research. The time_horizon_alignment_score is 0.8 due to a clear 20-year horizon, SIP step-up aligned with long-term growth, and infrequent rebalancing, indicating strong long-term orientation. The portfolio_activeness_score is 0.3: while two funds are index-based, the small-cap and flexi-cap funds show high tracking error (11.7% and 5.6%), positive alpha, and active management characteristics; the overall portfolio is weighted toward active management despite partial passive exposure.

pqs.reasoning: The investment plan is highly aligned with Captain Haddock's aggressive risk appetite, 20-year horizon, and financial goals (retirement, house, education). The 90% equity allocation via mid-cap, small-cap, flexi-cap, and US index funds supports long-term growth targeting an 8Cr corpus. Diversification is strong across market caps (mid, small, flexi), geographies (India + US), and fund houses, though slightly skewed toward aggressive segments. Risk-return is appropriate: high expected returns (19.5% base) match aggressive profile, with clear risk disclosures on volatility and drawdowns. The plan is internally consistent—rationale, allocations, SIP+lumpsum mode, rebalancing, and review checkpoints all align coherently. Tax efficiency is optimal: investor is in 'new_regime' (no 80C benefit), so ELSS is correctly omitted. All funds are equity-category (≥65% equity), ensuring LTCG/STCG tax treatment (12.5% above ₹1.25L, 20% below 1y) instead of slab taxation—ideal for tax efficiency. No slab-taxed debt funds are used, and no tax-inefficient hybrids are recommended. Thus, tax_efficiency is scored 1.0.
